commands.repair_reason: Protected security-boundary PR needs maintainer-reviewed authorization semantics and contributor real behavior proof before any merge path.; Needs attention: Needs attention because Discord target authorization can still proceed without platform-proven target guild membership.; Review finding: [P1] Require guild proof before allowing Discord read targets — extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.messaging.shared.ts:3... commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Discord and Slack allowlist checks for read-style message, reaction, pin, search, and file-download targets, plus regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main reaches Discord and Slack platform read APIs after resolving target IDs without independent target allowlist checks. No live transport reproduction was provided or run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected security-boundary PR needs maintainer-reviewed authorization semantics and contributor real behavior proof before any merge path.

target.title: fix(integrations): enforce channel read target allowlists [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord and Slack message read actions could target channels outside configured channel allowlists.
- Solution: Check the resolved read target channel against the owning integration allowlist before platform fetch/history calls.
- What changed: Discord `fetchMessage` / `readMessages` and Slack `readMessages` now reject non-allowlisted target channels; regression tests cover allowed and rejected targets.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Inbound message gating, write actions, platform permissions, and integration setup flows were not changed.

AI-assisted: Yes.

## Motivation

- Channel allowlists should bound both the channel that starts a turn and any channel-scoped read target used by message actions.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord and Slack message read actions now reject target channels outside the configured allowlist.
- Real environment tested: Not run by this agent; supervisor-owned verification pending.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Not run by this agent.
- Evidence after fix: Regression coverage was added in `extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts` and `extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts`.
- Observed result after fix: Source-level behavior rejects non-allowlisted read targets before platform read/fetch calls.
- What was not tested: Live Discord/Slack workspaces and package-manager-backed test commands were not run by this agent.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Message action read paths trusted model-supplied target channel IDs after inbound turn authorization.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing coverage did not assert that action target channels are checked independently from the triggering channel.
- Contributing context (if known): Read actions share bot/app credentials with broader platform visibility than the requesting user may have.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`, `extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Allowlisted read targets proceed; non-allowlisted read targets reject before platform read calls.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The action runtimes own target resolution and dispatch to platform read APIs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None found for action target allowlist enforcement.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord and Slack message read actions now fail when asked to read a target channel that is not allowed by the configured channel policy.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[allowed inbound channel] -> [read action target] -> [platform read]

After:
[allowed inbound channel] -> [read action target] -> [allowlist check] -> [platform read or rejection]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Message read actions now enforce configured target-channel policy before using integration credentials. This narrows data access. Operators relying on broad reads should explicitly allow the target channels they expect agents to read.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Not run by this agent
- Runtime/container: Not run by this agent
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord, Slack
- Relevant config (redacted): Allowlisted channel plus a separate non-allowlisted target channel

### Steps

1. Configure an integration with a channel allowlist.
2. Trigger an agent turn from an allowed channel.
3. Ask the message read action to target a different channel that is not allowlisted.

### Expected

- The read action rejects the non-allowlisted target before calling the platform read/fetch API.

### Actual

- Source changes implement the rejection; runtime verification pending.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Source-level review of Discord and Slack read target checks plus regression test coverage.
- Edge cases checked: Allowed target proceeds; non-allowlisted target rejects before mocked platform reads.
- What you did **not** verify: Live integration behavior and package-manager-backed test execution.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Existing workflows that read across channels without explicit target allowlist entries may now receive a rejection.
  - Mitigation: Add intended target channels to the integration channel allowlist.
